streams.pump_role: single feed stream containing all reagents premixed in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: All reagents are compatible at room temperature and premixing in DES is safe since reaction only initiates at 120°C. Single stream simplifies pumping of viscous DES medium.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: No mixing order constraints. Single premixed feed stream is optimal given high viscosity of DES (~50-100 cP) which would make precise T-mixer operation difficult with separate viscous streams.
post_reactor_steps: cooling to room temperature to stop reaction
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition proceeding via in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate followed by [3+2] cycloaddition with phenylacetylene. Key design considerations: (1) DES viscosity requires wider bore tubing (1.0 mm ID) and higher pump pressures, (2) 120°C operation requires BPR to prevent solvent boiling, (3) 2.0 equiv nitroacetate compensates for competing nitrile oxide dimerization, (4) calculations show Pe=9 indicating significant axial dispersion but acceptable for this reaction, (5) mass transfer limited (Da=39) but single stream eliminates mixing bottleneck.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 0.8 min (range 0.5-2.2 min) based on 20× intensification factor from analogies. Batch time 15 min reduced to 0.8 min in flow due to superior heat transfer.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: V_R/τ = 0.4 mL / 0.8 min = 0.5 mL/min. Single stream flow rate.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - required for nitrile oxide generation and cycloaddition. No adjustment needed.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation result: 0.067 M in reactor (diluted from batch 0.5 M due to flow dynamics). Analogy 1 used 0.5 M but different chemistry.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation required 5.0 bar. EG bp ~197°C but DES mixture may have lower effective bp. BPR mandatory for 120°C operation.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Coil reactor from Chemistry Plan stage 1. Analogy 1 used steel coil at high temperature - directly applicable.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: Stainless steel for 120°C operation. Analogy 1 used steel coil at 200°C confirming suitability. FEP/PFA not needed (no photochemistry).
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: 1.0 mm from calculation to handle DES viscosity. Analogy 3 used 1.2 mm for different chemistry. Calculation shows acceptable pressure drop.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation: 0.38 mL rounded to 0.4 mL. Residence time × flow rate verification: 0.8 min × 0.5 mL/min = 0.4 mL.
reasoning_per_field.light_setup: Empty string - thermal reaction only, no photochemistry involved per Chemistry Plan.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: null - no light required for thermal cycloaddition
reasoning_per_field.deoxygenation_method: null - Chemistry Plan confirms not oxygen sensitive, reaction proceeds under air
